blockingIssues.description: `packages/webapp/src/tools/bash-tool-allowlist.ts` still treats `ArithBracedExpansion` as safe inside substring arithmetic, so inputs like `ls ${FOO:0:${BAR:-$(whoami)}}` can execute command substitution under an allow-listed head. The current regression suite covers direct `ArithCommandSubst` but not this braced-expansion arithmetic variant.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated `.factory/library/architecture.md` to correct the allow-list substring-arithmetic note: the current walker rejects direct `ArithCommandSubst` nodes but still leaves `ArithBracedExpansion` unchecked, so future workers do not assume that path is already covered.

rejectedObservations.observation: The vendored `just-bash` README still implies the smoke test is the canonical type-level guard.
rejectedObservations.reason: out-of-scope: this is feature-local README drift rather than a services.yaml/library fact or an AGENTS/skill guidance update for the validator to apply.

rejectedObservations.observation: Pure utility-wrapper features still have conflicting live-browser validation guidance between the `webapp-worker` skill and `.factory/library/user-testing.md`.
rejectedObservations.reason: duplicate: earlier scrutiny rounds already captured this systemic guidance conflict, so round 4 adds no materially new evidence.
